survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall beneath the kelp park, 2 m high and smaller verticals slightly deeper were dominated by bryozoans, particularly Chartella papyracea, Bugula plumosa, Crisia eburnea, sponges especially Dysidea fragilis nd colonial ascidians.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed silted boulders with a hydroid (mainly Nemertesia antennina) and bryozoans (mainly Bugula spp. and Crisia spp.) turf with a wide variety of sponges and ascidians. A few algae were also present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Hydroids, ascidians and sparse algae on silty boulders in the upper circalittoral. Balanus crenatus relatively dense with patches of Morchellium argus and tufts of erect bryozoans and hydroids.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed circalittoral bedrock with a scour fauna of Sertularia argentea, Balanus crenatus and lots of Alcyonidium diaphanum colonies. Also many small patches of Esperiopsis fucorum. One limestone boulder with Hiatella arctica and Ciona intestinalis in the holes.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upward facing scoured upper circalittoral bedrock with a short turf of Tubularia indivisa, foliose red algae and sparse sponges, other hydroids and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Very tide-swept circalittoral cobbles with Sertuaria argentea, patches of Polyclinum aurantium, Aplidium densum, Nucella lapillus and abundant Urticina felina. Large patches of Tubularia indivisa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral very exposed, tide-swept bedrock with a dense turf of crisiids, Cellaria spp., sponges including Axinella dissimilis, Tethya aurantium and large Pachymatisma johnstonia, large patches of Gymnangium montagui and patches of Flustra foliacea. Also Alcyonium digitatum and large patches of Corynactis viridis amongst the hydroid/bryozoan turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Small cave (4 m long, 1 m high) with abundant Caryophyllia smithii on the roof and walls. Also sheets of thin encrusting sponges, especially Aplysilla sulfurea (both colour morphs), small patches of Thymosia guernei and Galathea strigosa in cracks and crevices in the roof.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR BardseyIsland and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder slope. Dominant (ie conspicuous when first examined) species include Nemertesia antennina, Esperiopsis fucorum, Sertularia argentea , Gymnangium montagui, Morchellium argus, and Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed silted boulders with a hydroid (mainly Nemertesia antennina) and bryozoans (mainly Bugula spp. and Crisia spp.) turf with a wide variety of sponges and ascidians. A few algae were also present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral very exposed, tide-swept bedrock with a dense turf of crisiids, Cellaria spp., sponges including Axinella dissimilis, Tethya aurantium and large Pachymatisma johnstonia, large patches of Gymnangium montagui and patches of Flustra foliacea. Also Alcyonium digitatum and large patches of Corynactis viridis amongst the hydroid/bryozoan turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sbulittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Vertical bedrock. Sponge and bryozoan turf dominated this biotope. Species include Pachymatisma johstonia, Dysidea fragilis and an orange sponge. Turf species include Bugula turbinata and Crisia denticulata. The sponge Dercitus bucklandii was present. Plants of Plocamium cartilagineum. There were patches of Corynactis viridis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bartdsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A large limestone outcrop. This description is restricted to an overhang. This was dominated by Pachymatisma johnstonia, Corynactis viridis and Polycarpa rustica. The cup coral Caryophyllia inornata occurred right under the overhang.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder slope. Dominant (ie conspicuous when first examined) species include Nemertesia antennina, Esperiopsis fucorum, Sertularia argentea , Gymnangium montagui, Morchellium argus, and Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Polymastia boletiformis, Hemimycale columella andT ethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstoniaa nd a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardseyo Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Gully sides and floors of tide-swept, exposed circalittoral. Tubularia indivisa covered walls with many Sagartia elegans. Of interest were patches of Dercitus bucklandi and erect growths of Haliclona oculata and a record of the sponge Stelleta grubii.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on bedrock. Main species were Laminaria hyperborea, Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea. Also conspicuous were the sponges Esperoipsis fucorum, Pachymatisma johnstonia and Hemimycale columellas. There was a fair bit of Morchellium argus too.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall beneath the kelp park, 2 m high and smaller verticals slightly deeper were dominated by bryozoans, particularly Chartella papyracea, Bugula plumosa, Crisia eburnea, sponges especially Dysidea fragilis and colonial ascidians.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral very exposed, tide-swept bedrock with a dense turf of crisiids, Cellaria spp., sponges including Axinella dissimilis, Tethya aurantium and large Pachymatisma johnstonia, large patches of Gymnangium montagui and patches of Flustra foliacea. Also Alcyonium digitatum and large patches of Corynactis viridis amongst the hydroid/bryozoan turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: S of Ogof Morla s(Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder slope. Dominant (ie conspicuous when first examined) species include Nemertesia antennina, Esperiopsis fucorum, Sertularia argentea , Gymnangium montagui, Morchellium argus, and Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A single bedrock/large boulder outcrop was studied. The rock was covered in Flustra foliacea (with the hydroid Laomedea flexuosa). Also conspicuous was Balanus crenatus and the ascidian Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Polymastia boletiformis, Hemimycale columella and Tethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with redalgae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Very tide-swept circalittoral cobbles with Sertularia argentea, patches of Polyclinum aurantium, Aplidium densum, Nucella lapillus and abundant Urticina felina. Large patches of Tubularia indivisa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders. A mixture of Tubularia indivisa, Sertularia argentea, Esperopsis fucorum and Pachymatisma johnstonia. Barnacles were copious under this turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Pninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lelyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Areas of overhang on a wall and large boulder holes were extensive throughout the site with dense Corynactis viridis and Caryophyllia inornata. Numerous species of sponge were also present and a thin turf of bryozoans in patches.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Dense turf of Tubularia indivisa also with patches of Balanus crenatus. Other conspicuous species included Halichondria panicea, Sagartia elegans and Crisia denticulata. The habitat was the upper surfaces of tideswept rock- very large boulders in fact.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island adn SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on bedrock with a dense understory turf of red algae (mainly Plocamium cartilagineum and Delesseria sanguinea), bryozoans (mainly Bugula flabellata, Crisia eburnea and Scrupocellaria spp.) and sponges (mainly Esperiopsis fucorum and Dysidea fragilis). Dyphasia rosacea was on the kelp stipes.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Hydroids, ascidians and sparse algae on silty boulders in the upper circalittoral. Balanus crenatus relatively dense with patches of Morchellium argus and tufts of erect bryozoans and hydroids.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral bedrock and boulders with Alcyonidium diaphanum, hydroids (mainly Diphasia rosacea and Sertularia argentea) and bryozoans (Crisia eburnea and Alcyonidium diaphanum). There were also abundant Balanus crenatus covering the rocks.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral bedrock with a Laminaria hyperborea park. This was not surveyed in detail but a red algae understory was present along with red algae on kelp stipes. The predominant red alga was Plocamium cartilagineum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crutss and Dysidea fragilis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Silty lower infralittoral boulders. Dominant species included Plocamium cartilagineum, Phylophora crispa and Drachiella heterocarpa. Animals included Balanus crenatus and Dendrodoa grossularia covering the silty rock. Others included Dysidea fragilis, Morchellium argus and Hemimycale columella.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Silty lower infralittoral boulders. Dominant species included Plocamium cartilagineum, Phylophora crispa and Drachiella heterocarpa. Animals included Balanus crenatus and Dendrodoa grossularia covering the silty rock. Others included Dysidea fragilis, Morchellium argus and Hemimycale columella.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock. Dominating the rather silty surface was dense Plocamium cartilaginum. The other conspicuous alga was Corallina officinalis. Characterising animals included Dysidea fragilis, Nemertesia antennina Morchellium argus and little growths of Stelligera rigida.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral surqvey
sample_comment: A boulder slope with a mixture of red seaweeds including Delesseria sanguinea and Laminaria spp. sporelings. Also hydroids with dense Diphasia rosacea and Sertularia argentea. Many Balanus crenatus (especially juveniles) present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Polymastia boletiformis, Hemimycale columella and Tethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Partially recorded kelp forest on a bedrock shelf above a wall with red algae, sponges and colonial ascidians.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on bedrock. Main species were Laminaria hyperborea, Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea. Also conspicuous were the sponges Esperoipsis fucorum, Pachymatisma johnstonia and Hemimycale columellas. There was a fair bit of Morchellium argus too.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A turf of bryozoans Flustra foliacea and Crisia spp., hydroids Nemertesia antennina and Aglaophenia spp. and sponges such as Polymastia boletiformis, Polymastia mammilaris, Hemimycale columella, Phorbas fictitius and Dysidea fragilis. Density of sponges high with a fine turf throughout.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: An exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on large stable boulders. There was an understorey of red algae including Delesseria sanguinea and Drachiella heterocarpa and a variety of ascidians. Common Balanus crenatus covered the rock.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Overhang in the lower infralittoral formed by boulders. Walls dominated by Chartella papyracea and patches of the sponge Dysidea fragilis and an orange encrusting sponge. Small barnacles and a fuzz of Obelia spp. dominated the rock beneath. Some plants of Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa were present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral silty boulders with a dense covering of Balanus crenatus and with Halidrys siliquosa, Dictyota dichotoma and foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Hydroids, ascidians and sparse algae on silty boulders in the upper circalittoral. Balanus crenatus relatively dense with patches of Morchellium argus and tufts of erect bryozoans and hydroids.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral bedrock with a Laminaria hyperborea park. This was not surveyed in detail but a red algae understory was present along with red algae on kelp stipes. The predominant red alga was Plocamium cartilagineum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Tideswept circalittoral bedrock pinnacle with a dense short turf of a mixture of Tubularia spp., Mytilus edulis, Polyclinum aurantium, Balanus crenatus, Nemertesia antennina and short bushy hydroids. Also Alcyonium digitatum and sparse sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed circalittoral bedrock with a scour fauna of Sertularia argentea, Balanus crenatus and lots of Alcyonidium diaphanum colonies. Also many small patches of Esperiopsis fucorum. One limestone boulder with Hiatella arctica and Ciona intestinalis in the holes.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsuyla sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on undulating bedrock. Characteristic species were Laminaria hyperborea and Plocamium cartilagineum. Algae were not well developed. Animals included Sabellaria spinulosa, Balanus crenatus and Esperiopsis fucorum. Of interest was the ascidian Aplidium densum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral boulders and cobbles with Laminaria hyperborea on the larger boulders and a dense turf of red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa, hydroids including Sertularia argentea, Halecium spp. and Aglaopheonia spp. and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A single bedrock/large boulder outcrop was studied. The rock was covered in Flustra foliacea (with the hydroid Laomedea flexuosa). Also conspicuous was Balanus crenatus and the ascidian Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Polymastia boletiformis, Hemimycale columella and Tethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Pennisula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bardsey Lighthouse B(ardsey Island)
sample_comment: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey kIsland and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella slimited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

sample_comment: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on bedrock with a dense understory turf of red algae (mainly Plocamium cartilagineum and Delesseria sanguinea), bryozoans (mainly Bugula flabellata, Crisia eburnea and Scrupocellaria spp.) and sponges (mainly Esperiopsis fucorum and Dysidea fragilis). survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upward facing scoured upper circalittoral bedrock with a short turf of Tubularia indivisa, foliose red algae and sparse sponges, other hydroids and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral rounded boulders and cobbles with sparse epifauna of Balanus crenatus, sparse Tubularia indivisa and a few crustose bryozoans and Pomatoceros triqueter.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical rock faces and gully walls with dense Tubularia indivisa, and patches of Corynactis viridis, sponges and hydroids. Also clusters of Aplidium densum and Polyclinium aurantium and patches of limestone (?) covered in Polycarpa scuba

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Very tide-swept circalitotral cobbles with Sertularia argentea, patches of Polyclinum aurantium, Aplidium densum, Nucella lapillus and abundant Urticina felina. Large patches of Tubularia indivisa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders. A mixture of Tubularia indivisa, Sertularia argentea, Esperopsis fucorum and Pachymatisma johnstonia. Barnacles were copious under this turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninusla sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Llen Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Areas of overhang on a wall and large boulder holes were extensive throughout the site with dense Corynactis viridis and Caryophyllia inornata. Numerous species of sponge were also present and a thin turf of bryozoans in patches.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Partially recorded kelp forest on a bedrock shelf above a wall with red algae, sponges and colonial ascidians.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Dense turf of Tubularia indivisa also with patches of Balanus crenatus. Other conspicuous species included Halichondria panicea, Sagartia elegans and Crisia denticulata. The habitat was the upper surfaces of tideswept rock- very large boulders in fact.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Tideswept, exposed circalittoral bedrock with little other than dense Balanus crenatus, a few Sertularia argentea and Tubularia indivisa. Also several very large Balanus balanus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Overhang in a 8 m long tunnel formed by boulders, big enough to swim through. Dominant species were massive growths of white Pachymatisma johnstonia. Between these are dense clumps of Tubularia indivisa, sheets of Corynactis viridis and underlying Balanus crenatus were the dominant species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Gully sides and floors of tide-swept, exposed circalittoral. Tubularia indivisa covered walls with many Sagartia elegans. Of interest were patches of Dercitus bucklandi and erect growths of Haliclona oculata and a record of the sponge Stelleta grubii.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Flustra foliacea, Nemertesia antennina, Alcyonum digitatum, erect bryozoans, colonial ascidians and Raspailia ramosa covered tide-swept, rugged upper circalittoral bedrock. Patches of barnacles replaced the bryozoan turf in places.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Tideswept circalittoral bedrock pinnacle with a dense short turf of a mixture of Tubularia spp., Mytilus edulis, Polyclinum aurantium, Balanus crenatus, Nemertesia antennina and short bushy hydroids. Also Alcyonium digitatum and sparse sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock with superabundant Tubularia indivisa forming a dense blanket over the rock. In amongst the Tubularia indivisa was Actinothoe sphyrodata, Myxilla spp. and a variety of red algae.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral very exposed, tide-swept bedrock with a dense turf of crisiids, Cellaria spp., sponges including Axinella dissimilis, Tethya aurantium and large Pachymatisma johnstonia, large patches of Gymnangium montagui and patches of Flustra foliacea. Also Alcyonium digitatum and large patches of Corynactis viridis amongst the hydroid/bryozoan turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed circalittoral bedrock with a scour fauna of Sertularia argentea, Balanus crenatus and lots of Alcyonidium diaphanum colonies. Also many small patches of Esperiopsis fucorum. One limestone boulder with Hiatella arctica and Ciona intestinalis in the holes.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed silted boulders with a hydroid (mainly Nemertesia antennina) and bryozoans (mainly Bugula spp. and Crisia spp.) turf with a wide variety of sponges and ascidians. A few algae were also present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limitd to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Laminaria hyperborea forest with a dense understory of Plocamium cartilagineum, coralline crusts, Phycodrys rubens on the kelp stipes, colonial ascidians and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral bedrock with a Laminaria hyperborea park. This was not surveyed in detail but a red algae understory was present along with red algae on kelp stipes. The predominant red alga was Plocamium cartilagineum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Dense turf of Tubularia indivisa also with patches of Balanus crenatus. Other conspicuous species included Halichondria panicea, Sagartia elegans and Crisia denticulata. The habitat was the upper surfaces of tideswept rock- very large boulders in fact.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Vertical bedrock. survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on undulating bedrock. Characteristic species were Laminaria hyperborea and Plocamium cartilagineum. Algae were not well developed. Animals included Sabellaria spinulosa, Balanus crenatus and Esperiopsis fucorum. Of interest was the ascidian Aplidium densum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock. Dominating the rather silty surface was dense Plocamium cartilaginum. The other conspicuous alga was Corallina officinalis. Characterising animals included Dysidea fragilis, Nemertesia antennina Morchellium argus and little growths of Stelligera rigida.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral boulders and cobbles with Laminaria hyperborea on the larger boulders and a dense turf of red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa, hydroids including Sertularia argentea, Halecium spp. and Aglaopheonia spp. and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder slope. Dominant (ie conspicuous when first examined) species include Nemertesia antennina, Esperiopsis fucorum, Sertularia argentea , Gymnangium montagui, Morchellium argus, and Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sulittoral survey

Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascdiians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Bedrock sloping steadily upwards dominated by a mixture of young, in the season, foliose algae and mixed hydroid species. Dominant were Schottera nicaeensis and Diphasia rosacea. Of interest was the hydroid Gymnangium montaguii. There was some shell gravel and scour with Flustra foliacea and Urticina felina present in some areas.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral silty boulders with a dense covering of Balanus crenatus and with Halidrys siliquosa, Dictyota dichotoma and foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock with superabundant Tubularia indivisa forming a dense blanket over the rock. In amongst the Tubularia indivisa was Actinothoe sphyrodata, Myxilla spp. and a variety of red algae.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral bedrock and boulders with Alcyonidium diaphanum, hydroids (mainly Diphasia rosacea and Sertularia argentea) and bryozoans (Crisia eburnea and Alcyonidium diaphanum). There were also abundant Balanus crenatus covering the rocks.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral bedrock with a Laminaria hyperborea park. This was not surveyed in detail but a red algae understory was present along with red algae on kelp stipes. The predominant red alga was Plocamium cartilagineum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Vertical bedrock. Sponge and bryozoan turf dominated this biotope. Species include Pachymatisma johstonia, Dysidea fragilis and an orange sponge. Turf species include Bugula turbinata and Crisia denticulata. The sponge Dercitus bucklandii was present. Plants of Plocamium cartilagineum. There were patches of Corynactis viridis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder slope. Dominant (ie conspicuous when first examined) species include Nemertesia antennina, Esperiopsis fucorum, Sertularia argentea , Gymnangium montagui, Morchellium argus, and Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A single bedrock/large boulder outcrop was studied. The rock was covered in Flustra foliacea (with the hydroid Laomedea flexuosa). Also conspicuous was Balanus crenatus and the ascidian Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical rock faces and gully walls with dense Tubularia indivisa, and patches of Corynactis viridis, sponges and hydroids. Also clusters of Aplidium densum and Polyclinium aurantium and patches of limestone (?) covered in Polycarpa scuba

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula subltitoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstonia and a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Areas of overhang on a wall and large boulder holes were extensive trhoughout the site with dense Corynactis viridis and Caryophyllia inornata. Numerous species of sponge were also present and a thin turf of bryozoans in patches.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Overhang/boulder slide with very dense brown Corynactis viridis, long Halecium beanii and Bugula turbinata. Numerous photographs will extend specie list.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Overhanging bedrock. Large Pachymatisma johstonia and areas of a yellow/brown encrusting sponge. Also sheets of white Diplosoma spongiforme were conspicuous. Elsewhere the rock was covered in bryozoan turf in which Crisia denticulata, Bugula tubinata and Bugula flabellata being the main species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on bedrock. Main species were Laminaria hyperborea, Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea. Also conspicuous were the sponges Esperoipsis fucorum, Pachymatisma johnstonia and Hemimycale columellas. There was a fair bit of Morchellium argus too.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on bedrock with a dense understory turf of red algae (mainly Plocamium cartilagineum and Delesseria sanguinea), bryozoans (mainly Bugula flabellata, Crisia eburnea and Scrupocellaria spp.) and sponges (mainly Esperiopsis fucorum and Dysidea fragilis). survey_name: 1997 MNCR aBrdsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed silted boulders with a hydroid (mainly Nemertesia antennina) and bryozoans (mainly Bugula spp. and Crisia spp.) turf with a wide variety of sponges and ascidians. A few algae were also present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on undulating bedrock. Characteristic species were Laminaria hyperborea and Plocamium cartilagineum. Algae were not well developed. Animals included Sabellaria spinulosa, Balanus crenatus and Esperiopsis fucorum. Of interest was the ascidian Aplidium densum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Silty lower infralittoral boulders. Dominant species included Plocamium cartilagineum, Phylophora crispa and Drachiella heterocarpa. Animals included Balanus crenatus and Dendrodoa grossularia covering the silty rock. Others included Dysidea fragilis, Morchellium argus and Hemimycale columella.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral bedrock. Dominating the rather silty surface was dense Plocamium cartilaginum. The other conspicuous alga was Corallina officinalis. Characterising animals included Dysidea fragilis, Nemertesia antennina Morchellium argus and little growths of Stelligera rigida.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral boulders and cobbles with Laminaria hyperborea on the larger boulders and a dense turf of red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa, hydroids including Sertularia argentea, Halecium spp. and Aglaopheonia spp. and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A boulder slope with a mixture of red seaweeds including Delesseria sanguinea and Laminaria spp. sporelings. Also hydroids with dense Diphasia rosacea and Sertularia argentea. Many Balanus crenatus (especially juveniles) present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997t MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Silty boulders and cobbles in the uper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral boulders and cobbles with Laminaria hyperborea on the larger boulders and a dense turf of red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa, hydroids including Sertularia argentea, Halecium spp. and Aglaopheonia spp. and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A boulder slope with a mixture of red seaweeds including Delesseria sanguinea and Laminaria spp. sporelings. Also hydroids with dense Diphasia rosacea and Sertularia argentea. Many Balanus crenatus (especially juveniles) present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout,e Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on bedrock. Main species were Laminaria hyperborea, Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea. Also conspicuous were the sponges Esperoipsis fucorum, Pachymatisma johnstonia and Hemimycale columellas. There was a fair bit of Morchellium argus too.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed circalittoral bedrock with a scour fauna of Sertularia argentea, Balanus crenatus and lots of Alcyonidium diaphanum colonies. Also many small patches of Esperiopsis fucorum. One limestone boulder with Hiatella arctica and Ciona intestinalis in the holes.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder slope. Dominant (ie conspicuous when first examined) species include Nemertesia antennina, Esperiopsis fucorum, Sertularia argentea , Gymnangium montagui, Morchellium argus, and Cellaria sinuosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittorale survey
sample_comment: Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper ciraclittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula subltitoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigo (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders. A mixture of Tubularia indivisa, Sertularia argentea, Esperopsis fucorum and Pachymatisma johnstonia. Barnacles were copious under this turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.***

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Flustra foliacea, Nemertesia antennina, Alcyonium digitatum, erect bryozoans, colonial ascidians and Raspailia ramosa covered tide-swept, rugged upper circalittoral bedrock. Patches of barnacles replaced the bryozoan turf in places.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on bedrock with a dense understory turf of red algae (mainly Plocamium cartilagineum and Delesseria sanguinea), bryozoans (mainly Bugula flabellata, Crisia eburnea and Scrupocellaria spp.) and sponges (mainly Esperiopsis fucorum and Dysidea fragilis). Dyphasia rosacea was on the kelp stipes.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed silted boulders with a hydroid (mainly Nemertesia antennina) and bryozoans (mainly Bugula spp. and Crisia spp.) turf with a wide variety of sponges and ascidians. A few algae were also present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral very exposed, tide-swept bedrock with a dense turf of crisiids, Cellaria spp., sponges including Axinella dissimilis, Tethya aurantium and large Pachymatisma johnstonia, large patches of Gymnangium montagui and patches of Flustra foliacea. Also Alcyonium digitatum and large patches of Corynactis viridis amongst the hydroid/bryozoan turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on undulating bedrock. Characteristic species were Laminaria hyperborea and Plocamium cartilagineum. Algae were not well developed. Animals included Sabellaria spinulosa, Balanus crenatus and Esperiopsis fucorum. Of interest was the ascidian Aplidium densum.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1n997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Laminaria hyperborea forest with a dense understory of Plocamium cartilagineum, coralline crusts, Phycodrys rubens on the kelp stipes, colonial ascidians and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bardsey Lighthouse j(Bardsey Island)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and corallkine crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstonia and a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Isvland and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders. A mixture of Tubularia indivisa, Sertularia argentea, Esperopsis fucorum and Pachymatisma johnstonia. Barnacles were copious under this turf.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maaen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella cilaita and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Flustra foliacea, Nemertesia antennina, Alcyonium digitatum, erect bryozoans, colonial ascidians and Raspailia ramosa covered tide-swept, rugged upper circalittoral bedrock. Patches of barnacles replaced the bryozoan turf in places.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Overhanging bedrock. Large Pachymatisma johstonia and areas of a yellow/brown encrusting sponge. Also sheets of white Diplosoma spongiforme were conspicuous. Elsewhere the rock was covered in bryozoan turf in which Crisia denticulata, Bugula tubinata and Bugula flabellata being the main species.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Kelp forest on bedrock. Main species were Laminaria hyperborea, Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea. Also conspicuous were the sponges Esperoipsis fucorum, Pachymatisma johnstonia and Hemimycale columellas. There was a fair bit of Morchellium argus too.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Vertical wall beneath the kelp park, 2 m high and smaller verticals slightly deeper were dominated by bryozoans, particularly Chartella papyracea, Bugula plumosa, Crisia eburnea, sponges especially Dysidea fragilis and colonial ascidians.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: A turf of bryozoans Flustra foliacea and Crisia spp., hydroids Nemertesia antennina and Aglaophenia spp. and sponges such as Polymastia boletiformis, Polymastia mammilaris, Hemimycale columella, Phorbas fictitius and Dysidea fragilis. survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on bedrock with a dense understory turf of red algae (mainly Plocamium cartilagineum and Delesseria sanguinea), bryozoans (mainly Bugula flabellata, Crisia eburnea and Scrupocellaria spp.) and sponges (mainly Esperiopsis fucorum and Dysidea fragilis). survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Exposed silted boulders with a hydroid (mainly Nemertesia antennina) and bryozoans (mainly Bugula spp. and Crisia spp.) turf with a wide variety of sponges and ascidians. A few algae were also present.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Pennisula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Laminaria hyperborea forest with a dense understory of Ploamium cartilagineum, coralline crusts, Phycodrys rubens on the kelp stipes, colonial ascidians and bryozoans.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: E of Bae y Rhigol (Bardsey Island)
sample_comment: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, rpedominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)
sample_comment: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
location_name: N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island nad SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

survey_name: 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey
